question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy what action his Department is taking to improve special educational needs provision in education, employment and training for people with learning disabilities.
answer_text: Question 1 - AQO 640/22-27My Department has a range of provision for young people with special educational needs delivered through Further Education Colleges, Universities and across our vocational training provision.  However, there are significant gaps in provision, and these have been worsened by the loss of the European Social Fund. This is not acceptable. Everyone should be able to access opportunities and fulfil their potential.I have therefore tasked officials to review the current provision and to provide me with recommendations to improve support for young people with special educational needs.  I have also asked my officials to examine legislative protections and bring back advice to me as soon as possible. It is my hope that both practical support and legislative protections can be strengthened in this mandate. This will require collaboration across Departments.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether his Department has considered providing a special educational needs statement for those post-19, similar to the provision of Individual Development Plans in Wales.
answer_text: I have tasked my officials with scoping the existing provision and providing me with options to improve both access and outcomes for our young people with special educational needs.  Assessing the relevance and effectiveness of approaches used in other jurisdictions will be an important aspect of this work.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether his Department will consider a review into how young people with a special educational needs statement transition into (i) further and higher education; (ii) training; and (iii) employment.
answer_text: The provision of clear and appropriate pathways for young people transitioning from education into adulthood is already a key theme within the Department of Education’s End to End review of Special Educational Needs (SEN).  My officials are fully engaged in the activity of the SEN Transitions Working Group, jointly chaired by Department of Education and Department of Health, which specifically aims to understand the experiences and perceptions of the transitions planning process.  In addition, I have tasked my officials to review this department’s current provision for young people with Special Education Needs and to provide me with recommendations to improve support.  I have also asked for advice on the legislative protections. It is my hope that both practical support and legislative protections can be strengthened in this mandate and I intend to speak to my Executive colleagues on how collaboration across Departments could support improve outcomes for these young people.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail any discussions he has had with the Minister for the Economy in relation to extending special educational needs statements beyond school age to support young people into further education, training and employment.
answer_text: The Department of Education (DE) acknowledge the need to support young people transitioning from education into adulthood and this is a key theme within the End to End (E2E) Review of Special Educational Needs (SEN).  To further this work, the Department of Education (DE) and the Department of Health (DoH) jointly chair a ‘Transitions Working Group’ which includes officials from the Departments of Economy (DoE) and Communities (DfC), alongside the Education Authority (EA) and the Public Health Agency (PHA).  The aim of the working group is to consider the current statutory transition process and associated practices, with a view to improving the experience for young people and their families while enhancing opportunities to ensure that there are clear and appropriate pathways into post-19 further education, training or employment provisions for those with SEN or disabilities.  My department is continuing to engage directly with practitioners, parents and young people who have experience of the transitions process to help inform recommendations on how we can improve pathways for young people aged over 19 years old with SEN or disability into further education or employment.  I would intend to engage with both the Minister for Economy and the Minister of Health in relation to setting out next steps once this process concludes.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether his Department plans to expand the number of physician associate roles.
answer_text: My Department’s officials have been working proactively with employers in the HSC Trusts to try to secure posts for the recent graduates from the MSc in Physician Associates programme at Ulster University.The current very challenging financial situation is impacting on Trusts’ ability to provide opportunities.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the steps his Department is taking to address the number of vacancies in the Children's Disability Community Team in Lisburn.
answer_text: My Department is aware that there are currently concerns regarding high vacancy rates within Lisburn Children With Disability Team. My officials have had communication with South Eastern HSC Trust, who advised that they have recently sent out an invitation to all service users, their families and local MLA’s to attend an information meeting to discuss the significant staffing issues that are being experienced within this service. The South Eastern HSC Trust are actively engaged in the regional Social Work recruitment processes, and also will be considering further local recruitment campaigns to attempt to acquire the social work staff to fill the current vacancies and alleviate the pressures experienced.  I am aware that there are current ongoing regional workforce pressures in terms of availability and recruitment experienced throughout children’s social work, and this includes the Children’s Disability Social Work Teams. The Children’s directorate is continuing to work in partnership with the Department of Health and Office of Social Services, in respect of safe staffing / workforce supply, and this includes Children’s Disability Services. My Department has worked collaboratively with HSC Trusts, Universities and Colleges, Trade Union representatives and other key stakeholders, in order to attempt to alleviate some of the regional pressures experienced, as a result of recruitment and retention issues in social work teams. A Social Work Workforce Review was undertaken in 2021 and its findings were published in 2022. The Department subsequently established the Social Work Workforce Implementation Board, involving senior leaders and a range of workforce representatives, to plan and deliver real improvements in the short, medium and longer term. 		There has been some progress made in alleviating pressures and improving stability in the social work workforce, including:In December 2022, a decision was taken to end the use of agency social workers in all HSC services by June 2023.The Trusts recruited around 160 agency social workers into vacant HSC posts between January and June 2023, and all use of agency social workers in HSC has now ceased. The Department created a faster, more efficient route into Trust employment for newly qualified social workers, which resulted in almost 200 vacancies being filled between June and September 2023. It is intended to repeat this exercise in the Summer of 2024.The Department has also increased the number of training places for student social workers by commissioning 25 additional places with the Open University NI. The Department continues to advocate for an increase in training places for student social workers, which is entirely dependent upon the availability of additional, recurrent funding.The Department will continue to monitor workforce vacancy rates across all five HSC Trusts within all service areas, and will continue to work with the HSC Trusts in terms of developing improvement plans.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail (i) the services provided by his Department to support those with eating disorders; and (ii) how these services range between the Health and Social Care Trusts.
answer_text: There are currently four adult Eating Disorder Specialist Teams across the five Health and Social Care Trusts.  The Belfast Trust and South Eastern Trust is represented by one team.  These teams have developed community based psychological therapy services using evidence-based treatments as per National Institute for Clinical Excellence (NICE) guidance.  They work to the Regional Care Pathway for the Treatment of Eating Disorders (2016). The care pathway outlines what people can expect if they are referred for treatment and provides summary of clinical advice derived from evidence-based practice. It also includes information to assist people with self-care and accessing other supports before, during and after treatment.Children and Young PeopleThere are currently four Eating Disorder Specialist Teams across the five Health and Social Care Trusts.  The Belfast Trust and South Eastern Trust is represented by one team.  These Specialist Teams are listed belowBelfast Trust and South Eastern TrustChild and Adult Mental Health Service - Eating Disorder Service Youth (EDYS)Northern TrustChild and Adult Mental Health Service - Eating Disorder Team (EDT)Southern TrustChild and Adult Mental Health Service - Eating Disorder Service (EDS)Western TrustChild and Adult Mental Health Service - Eating Disorder Service (EDS)EDYS, EDT and EDS within the Trusts work with children and young people who have been assessed and formally diagnosed with anorexia nervosa or bulimia nervosa. The service adopts specific criteria that is listed in the Diagnostic and Statistical Manual of Mental Disorders (DSM-5) to identify and diagnose. At this time these are the only eating disorders which are treated by this team. Once a young person is physically well and weight restored, the service offer talking therapy to help young people and their families. The team have formal training in psychological therapies including family therapy and in cognitive behavioural therapies.The Regional Eating Disorder Network Group (REDNG) ensures local developments are coherent and standardised as far as possible. Representation on the REDNG is lifespan. It includes individuals from Department of Health’s Strategic Planning and Performance Group/Health Policy, Public Health Agency, Health and Social Care Trusts including, Eating Disorder Service Clinical Leads, Team Leads and Clinical Service Managers, Psychology nominee (nominated on behalf of psychology services in all Trusts), experts by experience encompassing the voluntary sector, service users and carers, Trust service managers. The role of the REDNG is to promote the uniformity of approaches to patients across Trusts and share best practice and learning from service developments.  It provides a consistent and equitable approach across NI to the planning and commissioning of high quality, effective and accessible treatment for service users. Standards are based upon those laid out by the Royal College of Psychiatrists and NICE (National Inst

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice whether her Department will introduce legislation to amend the Rehabilitation of Offenders (Northern Ireland) Order 1978 to reduce existing rehabilitation periods.
answer_text: I remain committed to bringing forward legislative reforms to reduce existing rehabilitation periods during this Assembly mandate.  I intend to include legislative provisions in a Justice Bill to effect the changes to the Rehabilitation of Offenders (Northern Ireland) Order 1978 Order that I wish to deliver.  Subject to the Executive’s approval, I hope to introduce the Bill to the Assembly shortly.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail which recommendations from the High Street Task Force: Delivering a 21st Century High Street report his Department has responsibility for.
answer_text: The High Street Task Force was led by the Executive Office as chair, with support from the Department for Communities in a secretariat role. Implementation of the ‘Delivering a 21st Century High Street’ report recommendations will require Executive agreement and significant cross-departmental activity. Recommendations have not been formally allocated to Departments. I understand that Ministers in the Executive Office are currently considering how to take forward findings from the report.I believe my department will be asked to lead on the ‘Fair Work on our High Streets’ recommendation. I am proactively advancing work on this recommendation by developing a new Employment Bill.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy what further action will he take, in partnership with the Minister of Finance, to support small hospitality businesses.
answer_text: Small hospitality businesses play a vital role in our tourism sector and the economy more broadly. I recognise the importance of small hospitality businesses in delivering a regionally balanced economy, which is one of my Department’s key priorities.I am aware one of the key current challenges for these businesses is rising energy costs. Hence, my Department is taking steps to support businesses through delivery of the Executive’s ‘Path to Net Zero Energy’ Strategy, a key objective being to ensure that all consumers have access to essential and affordable energy. Tourism NI provides a suite of advisory supports to help tourism and hospitality businesses reduce costs, improve productivity and be more sustainable, including Leaner and Greener, and Innovate Tourism.The topic of hospitality and VAT has recently been debated in the Assembly and I know that the Department of Finance has repeatedly raised with the Treasury the negative impacts on hospitality of high levels of VAT. I will continue to engage with the Minister for Finance to push for reform of the VAT rate for our hospitality industry.	In March this year, I met with the NI Tourism Alliance to discuss concerns about the introduction of the Electronic Travel Authorisation Scheme. My Department is also actively working to establish a Tourism Partnership Board, involving Executive Departments, tourism and hospitality representatives and local authorities. The Tourism Partnership Board will develop and oversee an action plan to grow our local tourism sector.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on what actions his Department intends to take following the Independent Review of Education.
answer_text: The recommendations from the Independent Review of Education are still under consideration. I am looking forward to the Education Minister bringing forward a paper to the Executive in relation to the review and I look forward to further engagement on this.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the budget he will allocate to the relevant actions in the High Street Task Force: Delivering a 21st Century High Street report.
answer_text: The High Street Task Force was led by the Executive Office as chair, with support from the Department for Communities in a secretariat role.  All recommendations in the ‘Delivering a 21st Century High Street’ report will require Executive endorsement and a commitment to provide or redirect additional resources and funding, as required.I understand that Ministers in the Executive Office are currently considering how to take forward findings from the report.In the meantime, my department is proactively advancing the ‘Fair Work on our High Streets’ recommendation by developing a new Employment Bill.  This work is integrated into our routine departmental activities and does not have a specific budget allocation.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, following the budget allocation, whether the Skill Up programme will be funded.
answer_text: To align with my economic vision, I am considering the best means of closing the skills gap, including which programmes, and funding will be suitable to address this. The Skill Up Post Project Evaluation will be completed by 30th June 2024, and I will consider the appropriate targeted skills initiatives based on the evidence collated.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether he has approved a business case to extend the contract through DFI Roads Southern Area to enable white lining works to continue in the Armagh City, Banbridge and Craigavon Borough Council area.
answer_text: In accordance with the Public Contracts Regulations 2015 (as amended), an increase in the value of the term contract for the delivery of road markings within Armagh, Banbridge and Craigavon has been approved, to allow for the continued provision of this work until replacement contracts can be awarded in 2025.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail the number of primary school pupils that were referred to an education psychologist due solely to (i) moderate difficulty with mathematics; (ii) severe difficulty with mathematics; and (iii) suspected dyscalculia, in each of the last five years.
answer_text: The Education Psychology Service does not capture this data.  Schools use the Department of Education (DE) overarching Special Educational Needs (SEN) categories when referring to the Educational Psychology Service.  If a school has concerns about mathematics or literacy, they use the Cognition and Learning category.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether National Institute for Health and Care Excellence guidance (i) NG47; and (ii) NG35 has been implemented in Northern Ireland.
answer_text: My Department has a formal link with the National Institute for Health and Care Excellence (NICE) under which NICE Clinical Guidelines are reviewed locally for their legal and policy applicability here, and where found to be applicable, they are endorsed for implementation within Health and Social Care (HSC) organisations.  NICE Clinical Guideline NG47 - Haematological cancers: improving was endorsed by my Department for implementation in July 2016. NG35 - Myeloma: diagnosis and management was endorsed by my Department for implementation in April 2016. However, NG47: Haematological Cancers improving outcomes’, is not yet fully implemented in Northern Ireland.  Integral to compliance with NG46 is a Regional Specialist Integrated Haematological Malignancies Diagnostic Service (SIHMDS).  Implementation of SIHMDS is included as part of Action 15 of the Cancer Strategy for Northern Ireland 2022-2032. The NI Pathology network are in the process of developing a business case.  However, implementation will be dependent on identification of funding.	In respect of section 1.2. isolation facilities, work is ongoing with Belfast Health and Social Care Trust to progress a new 32 bedded haematology unit with the required isolation facilities, it is anticipated this capital build will be implemented 2028 at the earliest.  The development of an ambulatory care unit in Belfast Health and Social Care Trust is at an early stage and will require additional resources and funding.   With regard to paediatric services ambulatory care is provided for regimes such as Blinatumomab and cytarabine via the support of the CNS team carrying out home visits / care at home. Inpatient ward is all side rooms, however not all with ensuite therefore some patients will use shared bathroom facilities.  IPC measures are in place. NG35 - Myeloma: diagnosis and management- implementation is estimated at an average of 90 to 97% across all Trusts.  The only area of non-compliance relates to universal access to WBMRI which is considered gold standard.  There are also some issues with limited capacity in whole-body CT and PET CT in Trusts outside of Belfast. Many aspects of NG35 are now out of date and superseded by more recent guidelines.  All high intensity non-transplant chemotherapy patients are treated at Belfast Health and Social Care Trust clinical trials access has also been an issue.  Considerable work in last 18 months in Belfast Health and Social Care Trust resulted in the first clinical trial in some time opening with more planned later this year. To ensure implementation of this standard would require an increase in clinical trial workforce and expansion of access to whole body MRI. Progressing service developments required to fully comply with NG47 and NG35, is subject to regional commissioning and dependent on the complexity and scale for the development, available resource and other existing commissioning priorities.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, pursuant to AQW 11116/22-27, whether similar provision is available in all further education colleges.
answer_text: Following engagement by my officials with the colleges, I can confirm that all Further Education colleges offer the same provision and support in respect of Supported Learning. The Department assists Further Education colleges by providing funding to support students with learning difficulties and/or disabilities through the Additional Support Fund.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether she intends to develop a new lifelong learning project and action plan as outlined in Recommendation 28 of the Skills Strategy for Northern Ireland.
answer_text: A culture of Lifelong Learning is critical to the achievement of my economic vision; officials have been engaging with the Alliance for Lifelong Learning to consider how best my department can work with partners in support of this ambition.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure (i) whether the road marking contract limit has been reached for the 2024-25 financial year; and (ii) whether it will be increased to allow further work to continue.
answer_text: The Department has 8 term contracts in place to deliver road markings.There are no annual expenditure limits in these contracts.The road marking contracts do however have an estimated total value, which is set at the outset of the five-year contract period, and if a modification to this is required to enable work to continue, it will be done in accordance with the Public Contracts Regulations 2015 (as amended).

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, pursuant to AQW 11116/22-27, whether funding can be expanded to match the demand for the Centre for Supported Learning.
answer_text: For 2024/25, £4.5 million funding has been provided to Further Education Colleges under the Additional Support Fund. Following engagement by my officials with Belfast Metropolitan College, it has advised that whilst additional funding may expand course delivery, there would also be other constraints to expansion. These include the current mainstream funding model having limited ability to fund core programmes for those learners with significant additional needs, campus space availability, and due to the lack of multi-year funding, reliance on temporary staff resources which can be challenging in a tight labour market. 		My officials are conducting a review of all further education student support measures, including the Additional Support Fund provision for students with special educational needs, to ensure that the Department can effectively target student need.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy what action (i) her Department; and (ii) Invest NI will take following the news of the closure of the BT site in Enniskillen.
answer_text: I am very aware of the stress and worry the closure of the BT site in Enniskillen will be having on all those impacted.Officials at Invest NI continue to maintain close contact with the company and should redundancy clinics be required for staff Invest NI will work alongside the Department for Communities and Fermanagh & Omagh Council to deliver redundancy clinics to those impacted.At a policy level my Department will publish a Sub-Regional Economic Action Plan in the autumn. This plan will set an overarching framework of policies and programmes to tackle disparities, exploit opportunities, and deliver greater regional balance – one of my four key priorities. My officials and Invest NI are also continuing to engage with Fermanagh and Omagh Council, and other local stakeholders, to refine economic priorities and co-design interventions.  Invest NI is helping Fermanagh & Omagh Council to develop its regional proposition aligning sectoral strengths with sectors of opportunity and identifying USPs of the region to seek to attract inward investment.To encourage more export focussed start-ups and first-time exporters in the council area, particularly within Fermanagh, Invest NI has arranged monthly clinics at Enniskillen Workhouse to raise awareness of the support available to local businesses.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether the tourism action plan, which is under development, will be cross-departmental.
answer_text: Membership of the Tourism Partnership Board will be drawn from relevant Executive Departments as well as representatives of the tourism sector and the hospitality industry, and local government. This will enable cross-departmental engagement on relevant issues as part of the co-design approach to developing the Tourism Action Plan.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether the tourism action plan, which is under development, will be fully costed.
answer_text: I intend to publish a Tourism Vision and accompanying 10-Year Action Plan that will be co-designed and co-delivered with the industry. The Tourism Partnership Board will make recommendations to me on the final Vision and Action Plan, to help ensure alignment with my Department’s and the Executive’s broader economic plans. The Action Plan will be subject to the constraints of my Department’s budget allocation. There is currently only a single year budget. Within that, I have already made allocations to take forward key actions that I envisage being part of my Action Plan. I have allocated over £18.5m to ensure that my Department meets its funding commitment to our delivery partner Tourism Ireland, who is tasked with promoting Ireland as a destination for international visitors. I have also allocated £500k to Tourism NI to allow it to extend the Employer Excellence Programme into the North.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health, pursuant to AQW 8651/22-27, for a further update on the actions taken following the haematology pathway reviews.
answer_text: Officials are currently progressing the identified haematology pathway review actions.  As a first step an assessment of each Health Social Care Trust’s baseline position has been undertaken in respect of the recommended actions.   Where regional commissioning/investment is required, the timescale for implementation will be dependent on the complexity and scale of the issue, available resource and other existing commissioning priorities.Health will be facing significant budgetary pressures in 2024-25 with some extremely difficult decisions required to manage within the budget allocated. Therefore it may not be possible to progress all of the identified review actions within the existing budget.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on The Cutts junction road improvement scheme, Derriaghy.
answer_text: The Department is very aware of the public concern regarding collisions at this junction. The solution involves fully controlling the right turn movements within the signal phasing. I can advise that the procurement process is at an advanced stage and the scheme estimated at £2.5m is currently out to tender. Subject to the successful appointment of a contractor, construction is planned to commence in September 2024.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health, pursuant to AQW 7055/22-27, for an update on the business case for the regional centre for haematology at Belfast City Hospital, which was due for sign-off on 21 March 2024.
answer_text: The Outline Business Case for the regional centre for haematology at the Belfast City Hospital is currently being updated by the Belfast Trust. The Trust aims to resubmit the business case to the Department of Health for review by mid-June 2024.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health (i) whether the screeners involved in the Southern Health and Social Care Trust cervical smear recall have been working in other Health and Social Care Trusts; and (ii) whether an audit has been conducted.
answer_text: The Southern Health and Social Care Trust has advised it does not comment on individuals or their specific circumstances. However, the Trust has confirmed that none of the screeners involved are currently working within the Northern Ireland Cervical Screening Programme in any Trust.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure when the maintenance of white line road markings on the right-turn pocket into Ballyeden Meadows from Belfast Road, Magheralin, will be actioned.
answer_text: The refurbishment of the road markings at the Belfast Road/ Ballyeden Meadows junction Magheralin has been added to my Department’s programme of road marking work. This work will be carried out when the resources are available.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail the amount spent by the Housing Executive on temporary accommodation in Lagan Valley in the last three years.
answer_text: This issue is an operational matter for the Housing Executive and I have sought the requested information from the Chief Executive, who has advised me as follows:“The amount spent by the Northern Ireland Housing Executive on temporary accommodation in Lagan Valley in the last three years cannot be provided, as the data available cannot be broken down to this specific location.”For any future queries relating to operational matters, you may wish to contact the Housing Executive directly.  Making direct contact with the Housing Executive will ensure that you receive the information in the shortest possible timeframe and make best use of public resources.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance whether she will consider expanding the Small Business Rate Relief scheme to childcare providers with a Net Asset Value of more than £15,000.
answer_text: I would refer the member to the answer to AQW 8155/22-27.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister, pursuant to AQW 9409/22-27, for their assessment of whether this is value for money, given the reduced remit of the MLK Development Corporation due to the lack of ministerial direction.
answer_text: Expenditure is essential to secure and maintain the site, to discharge the duty to preserve the listed and retained buildings, to ensure the health and safety of those coming onto the site – including visitors to the Balmoral Show and the Ulster Aviation Society – and to facilitate the existing tenants on the site, such as Air Ambulance.The Development Corporation is subject to public expenditure processes and controls to ensure value for money.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister, following the adjournment debate on 23 April 2024, to detail when the MLK development corporation are scheduled to provide the Executive Office with advice and options on how to proceed.
answer_text: We have accepted an invitation to meet with the Board of the Maze/Long Kesh Development Corporation.  This will be an opportunity to hear from the Board about their thinking on development of a road map for the future.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, following the presentation to the Committee for the Economy on the Departmental Expenditure Limit Bids 2024-25, whether the recent budget allocation ensures the £18 million needed for apprenticeship programmes is available.
answer_text: I have reviewed priorities across my department and business areas have been issued with draft 2024-25 allocations for impact assessment and equality screening.   Whilst I anticipated that the proposed allocation will be sufficient to maintain the current apprenticeship provision, my final decision will be informed by the impact assessment and equality screening process.   The demand led nature of the programmes, which are tied to the wider labour market, may lead to fluctuations in future requirements.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health, pursuant to AQW 9195/22-27, whether he is aware that the Red Cross short-term wheelchair loans service is at risk of ending in Northern Ireland.
answer_text: I am not aware of this.  However, an official from my department haspreviously been in contact with the five HSC Trusts and they have provided assurances that they are liaising with the Red Cross in relation to this.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the number of (i) applications made to Belfast Metropolitan College's Centre for Supported Learning; and (ii) places that were available, in the last three years.
answer_text: Following engagement by my officials with the college, Belfast Metropolitan College has confirmed it had a total of 785 applications to its Centre for Supported Learning for the academic period 2020/21 to 2022/23. This comprised of 378 full time applications and 407 part time applications.  Further to this, Belfast Metropolitan College has confirmed there were 703 places available for its Centre for Supported Learning during the same academic period. This included 308 full time places and 395 part time places.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether he intends to introduce legislation to improve post-19 special educational needs provision.
answer_text: I do not currently intend to introduce legislation for post-19 special educational needs provision.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether he has met with the Minister of Education to discuss the delineation of roles between further education and schools.
answer_text: I recently met the Minister for Education to discuss shared priorities and the 14-19 Framework which includes an action for the Department of Education to review sixth form policy, recognising the importance of young people accessing the most appropriate learning environment for their career pathway.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy what action he is taking to expand special educational needs provision support in further education.
answer_text: Support for special educational needs within further education is in the form of the Additional Support Fund, and a review of all further education student support measures, including provision for students with special educational needs, is currently ongoing to help ensure support measures are being targeted as well as possible and on the basis of need.	I will be considering the scope to improve this support once my officials have concluded this review.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy how many of the 34 actions within the framework, Developing a More Strategic Approach to 14-19 Education and Training, have been progressed.
answer_text: From the 34 Actions set out in the Framework, 18 are classified as short term with a 3-year outcome. Of these 1 action is completed and 17 actions have been started and are in varying states of progress. They are being delivered by both DE and the Skills and Education Group in DfE.   Given the importance of this work, Minister Givan and Minister Murphy had recently met to discuss the delivery of the 14-19 Framework and other shared priorities. They reaffirmed commitment to our departments working collaboratively to support the educational opportunities for all our young people.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, pursuant to AQW 7199/22-27, why the new Careers Portal is taking so long to be delivered.
answer_text: The development of a new Careers Portal is an ambitious and complex IT enabled system transformation project. Successful delivery will help every part of the careers system to work together and improve access to high quality careers information and services.The project commenced with the allocation of resource to establish a project team in December 2023. The projected timescales are in line with industry best practice for a capital project of this complexity.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether he will develop bespoke skills action plans to support Northern Ireland’s key strategic clusters, as set out by Recommendation 24 of the Skills Strategy for Northern Ireland.
answer_text: My officials have been working with two business-led sub-groups of the Northern Ireland Skills Council to develop sector specific skills action plans for the digital and green energy sectors. I anticipate the Digital Skills Action Plan will be published in the coming weeks. The Green Energy Skills Group is working with my officials to develop an action plan, based on the findings of the ‘Skills Required to Transition to a Greener Economy’ Report, which was published in June last year and is available on my Department’s website.Scope remains to develop further sectoral skills action plans.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether he has met with the Minister of Education regarding improving careers advice in schools.

To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister whether they have published the detailed strategic review and revised strategy undertaken by the Maze Long Kesh Development Corporation, as noted in their Annual Report and Accounts 2022-23
answer_text: The strategy has not been published.  It is a working document, developed by the Maze/Long Kesh Development Corporation to illustrate what might be achieved on the site and inform future discussion.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister when they will answer AQW 8107/22-27.
answer_text: AQW 8107/22-27 was answered on 1 May 2024.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail the annual costs to maintain the former Dromore Central Primary School site, in each year since 2016.
answer_text: The annual costs incurred by the Education Authority to maintain the former Dromore Central Primary School site in each year since 2016 are as detailed below:Financial YearMaintenance Cost2016-17£6,859.052107-18£1,747.822018-19£6,531.692019-20£1,376.362020-21£4,708.552021-22£855.622022-23£6,680.222023-24Nil

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health when he will answer AQW 9195/22-27.
answer_text: The Department of Health does not hold a regional contract directly with the Red Cross to offer short-term wheel chair loans.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister when they will answer AQW 9409/22-27.
answer_text: AQW 9409/22-27 was answered on 24 April 2024.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to provide a timeline for the redevelopment of the Dromore Central Primary School site to be used for special educational needs provision.
answer_text: The Education Authority (EA) has a statutory responsibility to provide a suitable school placement for every child, including those with a statement of Special Educational Needs (SEN).  The EA has advised that the former site of Dromore Central Primary School is of strategic importance for the future provision of SEN places in the local area. On 30 April 2024 I announced a far-reaching programme of capital investment for children with SEN.  The new SEN Capital Programme will deliver up to eight entirely new special schools over the next 10 years, new builds for a number of existing special schools, an extension and refurbishment programme for special schools, accommodation for specialist classes in mainstream schools as well as additional maintenance and equipment funding.This additional investment will allow the EA to actively consider the feasibility of using the former site of Dromore Central Primary School to meet future demand for SEN accommodation.  Given the likely size and scale of the project, the Educational Authority has advised that this is a medium to long term objective, with a timetable available once the feasibility stage has been completed.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities when he will answer AQW 9411/22-27.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy (i) what discussions he has had with the UK government in relation to creating a Green Investment Zone in Northern Ireland; and (ii) when more detail in relation to this will be available.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health, pursuant to AQW 8344/22-27, whether Bevacizumab is available for patients with stage 4 liver cancer, as it is in other parts of the UK.
answer_text: My Department has a formal link with the National Institute for Health and Care Excellence (NICE) under which NICE Technology Appraisals are reviewed locally for their legal and policy applicability here, and where found to be applicable, they are endorsed for implementation within Health and Social Care (HSC) organisations. In practice, this means that treatments that have been recommended by NICE for routine use in the NHS in England are also available in Northern Ireland.In December 2020, NICE published TA666 which recommends the use of atezolizumab with bevacizumab for treating advanced or unresectable hepatocellular carcinoma which includes stage 4 liver cancer.I can advise that bevacizumab in combination with atezolizumab for treating advanced or unresectable hepatocellular carcinoma, where used as first-line treatment of advanced stage or unresectable cancer, was endorsed by my Department on 12th January 2021. Following consideration under the HSC Managed Entry process, this treatment is available to eligible patients in Northern Ireland in line with the current NICE guidelines (TA666). Further information on the treatment is available from:https://www.nice.org.uk/guidance/ta666 However, although available, this does not override or replace the individual responsibility of health professionals to make appropriate clinical decisions based on the individual circumstances of a patient, taking into account the clinical evidence available and in line with considerations around the potential risks and benefits of the treatment for the patient.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities whether he plans to introduce legislation to place an obligation on employers to protect employees from sexual harassment in the course of their employment, similar to that of the Worker Protection (Amendment of Equality Act 2010) Act 2023.
answer_text: The relevant provisions (sections 1-4) within the Worker Protection (Amendment of the Equality Act 2010) Act 2023 which reinforce the requirement for employers to tackle sexual harassment, will commence in England, Scotland and Wales on 26 October 2024.Amendments to the Sex Discrimination (Northern Ireland) Order 1976 would be necessary to ensure that the new requirements upon employers to tackle sexual harassment also apply in Northern Ireland.  I will be engaging with my officials in the near future to consider this matter and I will announce a way forward in due course.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to outline how much has been spent on the maintenance of the Maze Long Kesh site in the last five years.
answer_text: In the period 2018-19 to 2022-23, the Development Corporation has spent £606k on maintenance of the site.  This includes essential works to the listed and retained buildings, improvements to security, and grounds maintenance across the site.In addition, the Development Corporation has a separate programme of capital works.  There has been just over £3.6million capital expenditure in this period.  This includes significant repair and restoration of the World War 2 era hangars which house the Ulster Aviation Society collection.  It continues to prove popular and attracted some 3 thousand visitors over the European Heritage Open Day weekend in September 2023.  Improvements to access have also been carried out, to help facilitate the continued success of the Balmoral Show.We recognise the immense economic, historical and reconciliation potential of the site, and look forward to working with the recently appointed Board to maximise that potential.  We hope to meet with the Board over the coming weeks.  This will be an opportunity to take stock of the current position and hear from the Board about their thinking on the development of a road map for the future.  That discussion will inform consideration of the way forward and the potential timeframe.

The EA has confirmed there is a continued growth in demand for special education provision within the Lisburn and Castlereagh LGD, in primary and post-primary, for both special school places and specialist provision in mainstream schools (SPiMS) classes.  The EA has statutory responsibility for ensuring appropriate education provision for all children and is working at pace, in collaboration with managing authorities, sectoral support bodies and schools, to create additional capacity across the education system to ensure sufficient specialist education provision is available to meet the needs of children who require it for the 2024/25 academic year. Following written correspondence from the EA, the Council for Catholic Maintained Schools (CCMS) and support bodies, the EA is contacting individual schools in areas of pressure to seek their agreement to create additional provision for children with SEN who need it.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether his Department plans to create a young patient travel fund.
answer_text: Help with travel costs associated with medical treatment is provided via the Help with Health Costs (HwHC) scheme, which also provides financial help towards the cost of dental treatment, eyesight tests, glasses or contact lens.  The scheme is open to individuals and their dependants, primarily on low incomes. The eligibility criteria are set out in legislation and certain benefit recipients automatically passport on to the scheme.  Eligibility in terms of age is determined by the eligibility rules for benefits.Preparatory work has recently commenced in advance of updating the Travelling Expenses and Remission of Charges Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2004. The focus of the update is to reinstate automatic passporting for those eligible to receive free sight tests and free Health Service dental treatment through the Help with Health Care (HwHC) scheme following the migration of their benefits to Universal Credit by the Department for Communities (DfC).The commencement of a wider review of the provisions within these Regulations and associated HwHC scheme will be considered once this urgent work is completed and in line with available resources.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether he intends to conduct a public health campaign to raise awareness and encouragement for stem cell donation.
answer_text: The NHS Stem Cell Registry operates as part of a network of national and international registries which includes the British Bone Marrow Registry, Antony Nolan and DKMS, working together to match suitable potential donors with patients who require a stem cell transplant for blood cancer or other blood disorders. A number of charities in this space have very active UK-wide communications strategies and stem call donation campaigns which include promotion in Northern Ireland, which generally aim to raise awareness around the signs and symptoms of these conditions, and inform people about how they can join the registries if interested.  My Department has supported these campaigns in the past and I would be keen to do so in the future, however as you will be aware funding for any health-related awareness campaigns is regrettably limited at present due to wider severe budgetary pressures.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health, pursuant to AQO 106/22-27, for an update on the implementation of recommendations of the review of the three core haematological cancer pathways.
answer_text: My Department has recently undertaken service pathway reviews across the myeloma, lymphoma and leukaemia haematology pathways. The haematology pathway reviews have identified a number of areas where pathways and services would benefit from service transformation to improve service delivery and sustainability.The pathway reviews and reports were approved by Cancer Programme Board on 25 March 2024. This now provides an agreed strategic direction and next steps will include more detailed planning for implementation and the establishment of appropriate project management and oversight structures to oversee implementation.It is important that we ensure that the next steps are considered carefully to ensure that we can maximise the transformational impact of undertaking this important work.  I hope to be in a position to provide further updates on the progression of these activities over the coming months.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities whether he has any plans to enable carer's allowance payments beyond state pension age.
answer_text: It is a basic principle of the social security system that only one benefit at a time can be paid for the same purpose.  Even though the circumstances which give rise to entitlement to Carer’s Allowance and State Pension are different, they are both designed to provide a degree of replacement for lost or foregone income. Carer’s Allowance provides a measure of financial support for people who forgo the opportunity of full time employment due to caring for a severely disabled person. State Pension provides a degree of income replacement in retirement. 		It is possible that an individual over State Pension age can have entitlement to Carer’s Allowance and State Pension at the same time, however the Social Security (Overlapping Benefits) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1979 provide that they cannot be paid in full together. The payment of State Pension takes precedence, either reducing or extinguishing the amount of Carer’s Allowance payable.		Where Carer’s Allowance entitlement is established but cannot be paid due to overlapping benefit rules, the person will keep an underlying entitlement which may give them access to extra payments with other benefits.  For example, a carer’s premium in Housing Benefit or the equivalent additional amount for caring in State Pension Credit if they qualify for these income related benefits. 		There are no plans to change the overlapping benefit rules.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for an update on the proposed legislative framework for cancer registration.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether he intends to introduce legislation to implement training leave allowances for employees.
answer_text: Following a co-design and consultation process, it is my intention to bring forward an employment rights Bill within the current Assembly mandate. This will ensure that the employment law framework remains fit for purpose and operates effectively for both businesses and workers.  Training leave allowances for employees is an issue that I will consider as part of the Bill.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health, in relation to the issue raised by Secretary of State for Health and Social Care in relation to women in England who received radiotherapy above the waist to treat Hodgkin lymphoma not being given annual checks, whether this impacts patients in Northern Ireland.
answer_text: The Public Health Agency (PHA) has been informed by NHS England that a small number of women (less than five) who received radiotherapy to their chest for Hodgkin lymphoma in England, may have subsequently moved to Northern Ireland. PHA is liaising with NHS England and following these cases up locally to determine if these women currently reside in Northern Ireland, and if so, if they meet the clinical criteria for referral to the Very High Risk Breast Screening Programme. Any woman who is considered to meet the eligibility criteria will be contacted and invited to attend for screening.PHA has advised the Department that women who received radiotherapy treatment in Northern Ireland are not impacted by this issue.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy what representations he has made to the Secretary of State for Levelling Up, Housing and Communities to extend the UK Shared Prosperity Fund.
answer_text: The Finance Minister leads in negotiations on replacement EU funding with the British Government and I will continue to support her efforts in that regard.DoF continues to press for full replacement of EU funding and control over its use.The British Chancellor has confirmed that funding decisions for post-2025 Shared Prosperity Funding will be for the next spending review, to be conducted after the forthcoming UK general election, to consider.

answer_text: Effective, modern careers advice is vital to ensuring that people access the right skills and education pathways.  The Education Minister and I are due to meet to discuss careers provision, and this will include a discussion on the CAF and any potential role in a reformed careers ecosystem.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether cancer patients are being charged for the drug Bevacizumab.
answer_text: In Northern Ireland bevacizumab is commissioned for use within the Health and Social Care (HSC) system for a number of indications including: persistent, recurrent, or metastatic carcinoma of the cervix; and, platinum-resistant recurrent epithelial ovarian, fallopian tube, or primary peritoneal cancer in adult patients. Therefore HSC patients receive the treatment without charge.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health (i) whether he has considered enabling patients undergoing life-maintaining haemodialysis treatment, who travel independently to and from hospital, to receive full and timely reimbursement for their travel costs; and (ii) to detail the cost implications for his Department of doing so.
answer_text: The Help with Health Costs (HwHC) scheme provides a safety net for people on low incomes. Help from the scheme is income related and based on an assessment of each person's ability to pay. The scheme can provide help with HS dental treatment; sight tests; vouchers towards the cost of glasses or contact lenses; and with the costs of travel for treatment on referral by a doctor or dentist. Entitlement to payment of travel costs, is based solely on a person's financial circumstances and extends to the claimant's partner and dependant children. In the case where a refund is sought, entitlement to a refund is based on the person’s ability to pay on the relevant date.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether he is considering a review of student finance arrangements.
answer_text: I recognise the role of Higher Education in a thriving, regionally balanced economy; however, it is clear that there are significant financial pressures facing both the institutions and their current and prospective students.  I have asked my officials to update me on any work to date on a review of Higher Education Funding, which includes student finance, as well as proposals to take it forward.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether he will consider providing funding to Young Enterprise, given the nature of their work in encouraging entrepreneurship.
answer_text: Educating and inspiring the next generation of entrepreneurs is an important component of a modern, dynamic economy. We rely on a pipeline of young people coming forward, equipped with the skills, knowledge, and ambition to start their own business and drive economic growth. I am supportive of the work that Young Enterprise NI (YENI) does with our young people, and I have instructed my officials to continue to engage with the YENI team to see how we can work together to deliver my policy objectives.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether he is considering any changes to student finance arrangements to further support distance and part-time learners.
answer_text: Prior to my taking office, the higher education student support package for academic year 2024/25 had already been determined.I have asked my officials to update me on any work to date on a review of Higher Education Funding, which includes student finance arrangements for distance and part-time learners.  I will be engaging with my officials to consider the current support available and determine whether this can be improved.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail his Department's actions to improve care pathways for those with myalgic encephalomyelitis or chronic fatigue syndrome.
answer_text: NICE Guideline NG 206 makes a number of recommendations to improve care pathways for people living with myalgic encephalomyelitis or chronic fatigue syndrome (ME/CFS).It is estimated that the cost of developing services for ME/CFS in Northern Ireland in line with NICE guidance requires recurrent funding of £250,000.  Unfortunately, it has not been possible to secure this level of funding within the resources that are available at present. The current arrangements for the provision of ME/CFS services in Health and Social Care Trusts is summarised below:Belfast Trust provides an occupational therapy led ME/CFS programme for patients with mild to moderate symptoms;Northern Trust provides a self- management programme for clients with a confirmed diagnosis of mild/moderate ME/CFS. South Eastern Trust, Western Trust and Southern Trust offer support and symptom management through core services that best meet their individual needs.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, following the announcement of an additional £1 million financial support for higher education students, whether there will be additional funding for further education students.
answer_text: I provided an additional £1 million financial support for higher education students given the forecast from the institutions that there was a shortfall in funding to meet demand. Further Education colleges report that current student support funding is sufficient to meet demand. However, I have asked for a review of all current funding mechanisms for student support to consider how best to target need.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister, pursuant to AQW 5663/22-27, for a timeline on agreeing a future plan for the Maze/Long Kesh site.
answer_text: We plan to meet with the recently appointed Board of the Maze/Long Kesh Development Corporation over the coming weeks.  This will be an opportunity to take stock of the current position and hear from the Board about their thinking on the development of a road map for the future.  That discussion will inform consideration of the way forward and the potential timeframe.  We recognise in particular the importance of delivering a new access road connecting the site to the motorway, as a priority to enable regeneration of the site.We recognise the immense economic, historical and reconciliation potential of the site.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister, pursuant to AQW 5663/22-27, to confirm that without ministerial agreement on the future of the Maze/Long Kesh site, the Maze/Long Kesh Development Corporation is constrained to matters of health and safety, site security, essential maintenance and supporting the Royal Ulster Agricultural Society, the Ulster Aviation Society and the Air Ambulance Northern Ireland in accordance with agreements, leases and licenses.
answer_text: The Development Corporation is currently charged with maintaining and managing the site, carrying out essential health and safety works, and supporting the existing tenants.The Development Corporation seeks as far as possible to maintain a state of readiness for future development.  This includes continuing to consider possible approaches to regeneration of the site and provide advice and options for the future.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education whether all school buildings are compliant with the Disability Discrimination Act 1995.
answer_text: All new school buildings are built to a standard to be compliant with the Disability Discrimination Act (DDA) 1995.  The school estate consists of buildings of various ages and construction types and not all are DDA compliant however, where a minor work is brought forward to meet a pupil’s specific DDA needs these are prioritised and delivered in line with recommendations in the pupil’s Occupational Therapist report to ensure they have access to education and their needs are met.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, pursuant to AQW 5531/22-27, whether he will publish (i) his vision for further education; and (ii) an associated action plan on reforming the sector.
answer_text: A thriving, modern Further Education sector will be a key enabler for delivery of my economic vision.I will be engaging with the sector, including the Trade Unions, on how my department can support a sustainable sector in delivering high quality education and training across the region.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy what action he is taking to ensure a fair settlement is reached for further education lecturers on (i) pay; and (ii) terms and conditions.
answer_text: Further Education lecturers’ pay, as well as the terms and conditions of employment, are matters for the Further Education colleges as employers. I am committed to improving pay for Further Education lecturers and I understand pay negotiations have begun between FE colleges and Trade Unions on the 2023/24 lecturers’ pay agreement.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy (i) for his assessment of Belfast Metropolitan College removing itself from the College Employers’ Forum; and (ii) what action he his taking to ensure good industrial relations in the further education sector.
answer_text: I have been advised that Belfast Metropolitan College left the College Employers’ Forum in January 2024. I am committed to improving pay for Further Education lecturers and the priority is for 2023/24 lecturers’ pay settlement to be agreed and paid to staff. I understand that the six colleges are working to take forward and agree a collective pay award with Trade Unions. I understand that work is progressing with the six College Chairs and Principals on future collaborative approaches to managing staff-related matters and improve industrial relations in the further education sector.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, pursuant to AQW 6651/22-27, when the internal action plan will be published.
answer_text: My Department has currently no plans to publish the internal action plan referred to in AQW 6651/22-27.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance for an update on the implementation of recommendations from the NI Audit Office report on Public Procurement in Northern Ireland, published in April 2023.
answer_text: The NI Audit Office Report on Public Procurement in NI, published in April 2023 recommended a fundamental review to ensure that procurement structures support effective strategy setting, policy development, governance and accountability for procurement. 	In the absence of an Executive, the Department of Finance responded to the NIAO Report on Public Procurement in May 2023 stating that it accepted a review of procurement governance structures is necessary but advised that, as procurement was a cross cutting matter, this decision must be taken forward by the Minister for Finance and agreed by the Executive.Officials are working on a Terms of Reference for the review of Public Procurement.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy (i) whether delivery of the current Skills Strategy has been paused; and (ii) when an updated strategy will be completed.
answer_text: The delivery of the current Skills Strategy has not been paused and there are no plans to develop an updated strategy. However, I will be engaging with officials on how best to align and target efforts to ensure delivery of my economic vision.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether his Department has any plans to increase cross-border partnerships for further education colleges.
answer_text: Further Education colleges actively build and maintain collaborative cross-border relationships at both a sector and college level. My officials are also engaged with departmental counterparts and with Solas, the agency which oversees the Further Education and Training sector in the south.More formal partnerships include, for example, the North West Tertiary Education Cluster, which is a successful partnership between North West Regional College, Ulster University, Atlantic Technological University and the Donegal Education Training Board.I am keen to encourage more of this type of work to benefit our learners and a strong, regionally balanced economy.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education whether he plans to restore funding to Young Enterprise NI.
answer_text: My Department provided funding to Young Enterprise Northern Ireland over many years to deliver its enterprise education programmes in our schools.  Unfortunately, due to the challenging budget position in 2023-24, funding to the organisation ceased on 31 May 2023.With regard to the future funding of any programme, you will be aware that departments have not yet been advised of budget allocations for 2024-25.  While my Department has been advised of an indicative baseline for planning purposes, detailed budgetary planning and prioritisation has yet to be undertaken.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy what plans he has to improve the financial support available for carers to access further and higher education.
answer_text: Access to Further and Higher education is key for addressing equality of opportunity and creating pathways to good jobs. . This is particularly important for those who experience additional barriers to participation, such as people with caring responsibilities. To that end, a  range of financial support is currently available including FE Grants, Hardship Fund, Care to Learn (NI) childcare scheme, Childcare Grant, Adult Dependants Grant and Parental Learning Allowance. However, I am very mindful of the current cost of living pressures, and I will be  engaging with my officials to consider the current support available and determine whether this can be improved.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether he has plans to introduce similar legislation to that of the Strikes (Minimum Service Levels) Act 2023.
answer_text: No.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether all staff working in accommodation where young people are placed are Access NI checked.
answer_text: Unfortunately, as a result of significant pressures in accommodation provision, Health and Social Care Trusts occasionally have to place young people in unregulated accommodation, such as hotels or bed and breakfast accommodation. Such placements should be used rarely, in emergency circumstances only if another placement option is not immediately available, and for the shortest timeframe possible. There are a number of measures which should be taken to safeguard a child placed in a hotel or bed and breakfast. There should be a bespoke package of support for the young person; the accommodation must be registered with the NI Tourist Board; and a police check should be completed on the landlord. The subject of the check must have consented to a check being carried out, unless there is a clear rationale for not obtaining consent (for example, where doing so may place a child at risk). The Department is clear that no child under the age of 16 should be placed in unregulated accommodation.Under the statutory framework for disclosure checks undertaken by AccessNI, generally staff working in children’s homes and institutions exclusively or mainly for the detention of children are eligible for enhanced disclosure checks. Where eligibility for an enhanced check does not exist, a basic check is required.Currently, staff working in a hotel or bed and breakfast would not be eligible for an enhanced disclosure check, as they are not engaged in “regulated activity” as defined in the Safeguarding Vulnerable Groups (NI) Order 2007.  There is no legislative barrier to seeking a basic Access NI check in respect of these staff.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy (i) for an update on the issue of Mutual Recognition of Professional Qualifications; and (ii) whether he has any plans to address this issue.
answer_text: The Mutual Recognition of Professional Qualifications (MRPQ) is a cross-cutting issue which impacts upon a number of Departments. To promote a consistent and co-ordinated organisational response to these issues across the NICS a cross-departmental MRPQ Working Group, chaired by my officials, has been developed to monitor and oversee this work.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether Health and Social Care Trusts are placing young people in unregulated accommodation.
answer_text: Unfortunately, as a result of significant pressures in accommodation provision, Health and Social Care Trusts occasionally have to place young people in unregulated accommodation, such as hotels or bed and breakfast accommodation. Such placements should be used rarely, in emergency circumstances only if another placement option is not immediately available, and for the shortest timeframe possible. There are a number of measures which should be taken to safeguard a child placed in a hotel or bed and breakfast. There should be a bespoke package of support for the young person; the accommodation must be registered with the NI Tourist Board; and a police check should be completed on the landlord. The subject of the check must have consented to a check being carried out, unless there is a clear rationale for not obtaining consent (for example, where doing so may place a child at risk). The Department is clear that no child under the age of 16 should be placed in unregulated accommodation.Under the statutory framework for disclosure checks undertaken by AccessNI, generally staff working in children’s homes and institutions exclusively or mainly for the detention of children are eligible for enhanced disclosure checks. Where eligibility for an enhanced check does not exist, a basic check is required.Currently, staff working in a hotel or bed and breakfast would not be eligible for an enhanced disclosure check, as they are not engaged in “regulated activity” as defined in the Safeguarding Vulnerable Groups (NI) Order 2007.  There is no legislative barrier to seeking a basic Access NI check in respect of these staff.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether the implementation of Encompass has caused a delay in red flag referrals.
answer_text: Encompass was launched in the South Eastern Health and Social Care Trust on 9 November 2023, and as with the implementation of any new information system there will always be a period of reduced capacity, allowing for the system to become embedded and for staff to be trained.In anticipation of this predicted downturn in activity in South Eastern Trust, there was a planned redirection of red flag referrals for a small number of key tumour sites to other Health and Social Care Trusts and the Independent Sector for five working days to mitigate the risk of patient delays.  The South Eastern Trust have however identified an issue with the migration of data during the Go Live of encompass, which may have resulted in a number of patients referred for suspect cancer being delayed. All patients impacted are being prioritised and contacted by the Trust to confirm an appointment. GPs have also been advised that should they have concerns about a patient who was referred as a red flag referral and who has not received a first appointment, to email a dedicated email address within the Trust to advise. By way of an additional layer of assurance all GP red flag referrals from 24 October 2023 to 15 February 2024 have been validated and the Trust can confirm that all these referrals have been received into the encompass system.The Trust is actively working with encompass colleagues to prevent a recurrence at future go lives in the other HSC Trusts.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether his Department will consider developing a specific green skills barometer.
answer_text: My Department has published an audit of the skills and training needs required to deliver the targets set out in the Energy Strategy and commitments in the Climate Change Act. An industry reference group has been established to oversee this work and will also work with my officials to develop an action plan.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy what representations his Department has made to the UK government regarding the need to reform the Apprenticeship Levy in Northern Ireland.
answer_text: The Apprenticeship Levy is a reserved matter and my Department has no remit over either the paying or allocation of levy funds.  However, the Fiscal Commission recommended that the Apprenticeship Levy be devolved and this will form part of negotiations on a Fiscal Framework.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy when the careers portal project will be complete.
answer_text: A modern, collaborative careers service is critical for supporting people into good jobs. The development of a new Careers Portal will be key to this.  In line with best practice, officials are engaging with end users to fully define the requirements for the Portal; it is anticipated that the Engagement and Discovery phases will run until September 2024. Once the end product is fully defined, it will take between 18 and 24 months to deliver the project, subject to the availability of resources.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister for an update following the recommendations from the High Streets Task Force.
answer_text: The High Streets Taskforce’s report ‘Delivering a 21st Century High Street’ was presented to former Junior Ministers in March 2022 and includes recommendations that will be of interest to a number of Departments.We intend to write to Executive colleagues asking them to consider the way forward in relation to findings of the report.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health what actions his Department intends to take following the publication of the Hughes Report on 7 February 2024.
answer_text: My Department is aware of publication of the Hughes Report by the Patient Safety Commissioner in England and will take time to consider its findings and recommendations.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether the business case for the regional centre for haematology at Belfast City Hospital will be signed off for the projected timescale of 21 March 2024.
answer_text: The Belfast Trust’s business case for the new regional haematology ward at the Belfast City Hospital is currently under review by the Strategic Planning and Performance Group (SPPG). Due to the substantial increase in revenue costs linked to the capital build and service developments, an assessment of these costs is currently being carried out by SPPG and they are working with the Trust to finalise costs as soon as possible considering the current deadline.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health what support is available for patients with blood cancer.
answer_text: All blood cancer patients are managed in line with recognised clinical pathways. The haematology multi-disciplinary team (MDT) consists of a wide range of professionals all of whom support the patient as required. Patients eligible for transplant are referred onto Belfast Trust who provide this service for the region. All patients have access to a 24-hour helpline should they experience any concerns related to their treatment or condition.  All patients have access to a clinical nurse specialist (CNS) or key worker who assists to support them throughout their journey from the time of diagnosis. The patient’s CNS will explain the diagnosis and the Plan of Care. All newly diagnosed patients are provided with an information pack which highlights sources of support and patients are also supported with written information regarding their diagnosis and treatment. Holistic Needs Assessment (HNA) is also offered to patients. Holistic Needs Assessment allows the patient and their CNS to talk through a patient’s needs and concerns. The patient and CNS then agree on a plan for the patient’s care and support needs. In most Trusts patients on active monitoring have regular review carried out by a clinical nurse specialist (CNS).  This pathway supports the patient and facilitates rapid access to the consultant should this be required.    Social work staff are available for patient support; this can range from assistance with finances to psychological support. In Belfast trust which provides a tertiary service for specialist treatment there is also a physical activity coach who supports patients as needed, helping them to maintain their physical strength with exercise.  In addition, there are Health and Wellbeing sessions for patients specific to their haematological malignancy in most Trusts. These sessions are held periodically and patients are encouraged to attend as the events not only provide information and support but also an opportunity to meet other haematology patients.All cancer patients and their families are signposted or referred to the Trusts Macmillan Health and Wellbeing service which can refer the patients and carers to available relevant support services and groups including counselling services available through Cancer Focus. Patients also have access to Macmillan Welfare Rights review for financial advice/support. These Trust support services also provide a listening ear, and further signposting to Trust services and community/voluntary sector services, both for the person diagnosed and those who care for and support them.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health what his Department is doing to raise awareness of blood cancer.
answer_text: Through the “Be Cancer Aware” campaign and website the Public Health Agency (PHA) seeks to raise general cancer awareness around signs and symptoms, as well as raising awareness of cancer screening programmes. The PHA also utilises topical opportunities such as World Cancer Day and Cervical Cancer Prevention Week to further raise public awareness. This includes information on some forms of blood cancer.The PHA reviews information on a regular basis and will continue to develop the “Be Cancer Aware” campaign and website in line with the evidence base and as resources allow.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether Invest NI are in discussions with BT, following the announcement of the potential call centre closure in Enniskillen.
answer_text: I can confirm that Invest NI has met with BT concerning the announcement by the company of its intention to undertake a review of its customer services site in Enniskillen. The company has assured Invest NI that no decision has been made on the future of the BT Enniskillen contact centre pending completion of its review. Invest NI will continue to work with the company over the coming weeks to explore all opportunities to influence, where possible, the best possible outcome for the staff who may be impacted by any decision taken by BT.
