question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether his Department is considering undertaking an impact assessment following the UK Government's December 2023 announcements on migration policy, including increasing the minimum salary needed for skilled overseas workers.
answer_text: The current Points-Based Immigration System does not serve the needs of the economy here and I plan to engage with British Government Ministers on this policy, including the level at which the minimum salary threshold is set.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy (i) what group or body is overseeing the implementation of the action plan of the Independent Review of Invest NI; and (ii) whether there are members on that group from outside his Department and Invest NI.
answer_text: My department has established an Implementation Oversight Group (IOG) to provide strategic oversight and assurance on the implementation of the Invest NI Action Plan. IOG is a joint departmental and Invest NI forum, chaired by a senior departmental official. Its membership includes a senior official from The Executive Office who provides an independent challenge function as a Critical Friend. During its development phase, the Action Plan was also critically examined by key stakeholders, including the Independent Review Panel, through a series of external calibration sessions.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether he plans to publish the 14 to 19 Strategy action plan.
answer_text: I refer to my previous answer 6214 22-27. My Department is jointly responsible for delivery of the 14-19 Education and Training Framework along with the Department of Education. The Framework, including 34 strategic actions was published in 2022, however, the underpinning draft action plan was held as an internal document for officials to aid implementation. I will discuss next steps on implementation of the Framework with Minister Givan in due course.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy what plans he has to maximise the opportunities of dual market access.
answer_text: My officials will be examining all possible avenues to ensure the unique dual market access enjoyed here is fully realised.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether he will introduce legislation to ensure unpaid leave for carers.
answer_text: Following a co-design and consultation process, it is my intention to bring forward an employment rights Bill within the current Assembly mandate to ensure the employment law framework remains fit for purpose and operates effectively for both businesses and workers. Carer’s Leave is an issue I will be happy to consider in that context.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy how the Skills for a 10x Economy strategy will be used to deliver on climate targets.
answer_text: The Skills Strategy recommended that the department commission an audit of the skills and training needs required to deliver the targets as set out in the Energy Strategy and commitments included in the Climate Change Act. An industry reference group has been established to oversee this work and will also work with my officials to develop an action plan.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the number of consultant haematologist vacancies.
answer_text: At 31 December 2023, there were 3 consultant haematologist vacancies actively being recruited in Health and Social Care (HSC).  This information is published on a quarterly basis at the following link: https://www.health-ni.gov.uk/articles/staff-vacancies

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy what plans his Department has to increase the numbers of businesses offering apprenticeships.
answer_text: As responsibility for apprenticeships policy falls within the remit of my department, this question has been passed to me to respond.  In support of employers creating apprenticeship opportunities, my department provides funding for the delivery of apprenticeship training and oversees the operation of the apprenticeship training delivery infrastructure.  Associated training is delivered by further education colleges, higher education institutions, the College of Agriculture, Food and Rural Enterprise and non-statutory contracted training suppliers. Training at levels 2 and 3 is funded through the ApprenticeshipsNI programme, with the training from level 4 to 7 funded through the Higher Level Apprenticeship programme.  Both programmes cover a wide range of occupational areas.In addition, my department manages the delivery of apprenticeship promotional campaigns which help to highlight the benefits of apprenticeships to both potential apprentices and employers. These twice yearly advertising campaigns, which are across a range of media, take place during Apprenticeship Week in January/February and again in the summer for GCSE / AS / A2 results. The annual Apprenticeship Week, which this year ran from 5 to 9 February 2024, is also a great opportunity to highlight apprenticeships to would be apprentices and employers.  In particular, it offers the opportunity for employers to hear directly from those businesses which are actively recruiting apprentices and to understand the associated benefits apprenticeships bring.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy what plans his Department has to work with the Department of Health to support its workforce development.
answer_text: The Department has implemented a range of improvements and initiatives over the last two years as part of an Organisational Development and Transformation Programme. The Department has liaised closely with NICS HR throughout for advice. The then, Permanent Secretary, Mike Brennan, wrote to all Departmental Permanent Secretaries on 6 June 2023 offering to share lessons learned and any of the outputs from Programme with colleagues. Advice and assistance has been provided on request to a number of Departments including the Department of Health.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail (i) the reason for the continued signal failure at the Knockmore Road and Ballinderry Road junction, Lisburn; and (ii) when this issue will be resolved.
answer_text: Officials in my department are aware of the intermittent fault at this location and have been working with our contractors to identify the issue.A faulty component was identified as the cause of the recent failures and has now been replaced which should prevent any further reoccurrence.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities whether he will provide funding to help establish new Business Improvement Districts.
answer_text: I am very supportive of Business Improvement Districts and I acknowledge the potential of BIDs to be powerful and innovative tools for the regeneration of our town and city centres.My Department has responsibility for the policy and legislation for Business Improvement Districts, and has, to date, supported 8 BIDs through their development and ballot processes.My Department provided £450,000 in financial assistance to those 8 existing BIDs to support them with running costs during the Covid pandemic. My officials continue to be engaged with BIDs on policy and operational matters. I am aware that my officials have also engaged in recent months with several new groups of businesses and business representatives across Northern Ireland who have expressed interest in establishing a Business Improvement District for their local area.I fully appreciate that the cost of developing a BID proposal can in some cases be a barrier to their creation, and my officials are actively assessing the potential for any assistance my Department might be able to provide to these groups in the future.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure when the assessment of three pedestrian crossing locations in Royal Hillsborough will be completed.
answer_text: Officials in my department are undertaking a survey on pedestrian movement at three locations in Hillsborough.  The conclusion of this survey, in March 2024, will inform current proposals for an environmental improvement scheme to be implemented by Lisburn and Castlereagh City Council.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the current waiting times for breast cancer red flag referrals, broken down by Health and Social Care Trust.
answer_text: As part of the Cancer Waiting Times publications which are released quarterly by the Department, the length of wait from an urgent GP referral for suspect breast cancer to first appointment with a breast cancer specialist is monitored and reported on.  The most recent publication was published on 11th January 2024.  This can be found at the following link:Northern Ireland waiting time statistics: cancer waiting times July - September 2023 | Department of Health (health-ni.gov.uk).The next publication will be issued on Thursday 28th March 2024.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether his Department will prioritise the completion of the A1 Junctions Phase 2 scheme.
answer_text: Major Road schemes are an important part of the work that is delivered by my Department as we seek to reduce journey times increase reliability. and improve road safety.I remain fully committed to delivering this important road safety project.  The statutory Orders for the scheme were made in summer 2021 and work on the project is continuing through the development of contract documents and Business Case. In recognition of current and future potential funding challenges, my Department is exploring options to deliver the project in smaller phased packages.  Details of the current status and timescales for of the A1 Junctions Phase 2 project will continue to be available on the project webpage which can be viewed at: https://www.infrastructure-ni.gov.uk/articles/a1-junctions-phase-2-overview

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure when the assessment of alternative routes, due to the issue of a weight limit in Royal Hillsborough, will be completed.
answer_text: Officials in my department are committed to delivering an assessment on alternative routes for HGVs, to inform consideration of the possible introduction of a proposed weight limit in Royal Hillsborough.  This assessment is planned for completion in mid 2024.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities for a breakdown of the social housing waiting list in Lagan Valley for the last three years.
answer_text: Any breakdown of the social housing waiting list is an operational matter for the Housing Executive.You should, therefore, direct your question to the Housing Executive at its dedicated MLA response team, which can be contacted at public.affairsunit@nihe.gov.uk in relation to your specific query.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education for an update on the redevelopment of the former Dromore Central Primary School site.
answer_text: The Education Authority has advised that the former Dromore Central Primary School site is currently under consideration for potential Special Educational Needs purposes.Future development of this site will be subject to the relevant approvals and funding availability.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities how many social housing applicants with a disability are in need of accessible housing in Lagan Valley.
answer_text: The number of social housing applicants with a disability in need of accessible housing in Lagan Valley is an operational matter for the Housing Executive.You should, therefore, direct your question to the Housing Executive at its dedicated MLA response team, which can be contacted at public.affairsunit@nihe.gov.uk.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for his assessment of how the voluntary redundancy programme in Further Education is being carried out.
answer_text: I am aware that Further Education Colleges are working on a tailored staff exit scheme in 2023/24 based on the prioritisation of staff categories by College, depending on the base position and business need of each. I am currently taking advice from my officials on the options available to me for the future of the scheme.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister for an update on the development of the Maze/Long Kesh site.
answer_text: A new board was appointed on 30 October 2023.  We recognise the potential of the site and look forward to working with the new Board to see the site developed and its regeneration taken forward.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy what policy he will pursue in relation to a lifetime guarantee of training to level 2.
answer_text: A culture of lifelong learning is central to improving productivity and creating a stronger, more balanced economy; I will be engaging with my officials in the weeks ahead to determine how best to deliver that.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy what progress has been made on the transformation proposals for Further Education.
answer_text: A thriving Further Education sector is critical to a strong and regionally balanced economy. I have asked my officials for an update on the work carried out to date, and options for next steps, including how student numbers could be increased across the sector.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether he will introduce a ring-fenced skills fund as recommended in the Skills Strategy.
answer_text: As identified by the OECD, long term strategic investment in our skills system is essential to achieving our social and economic objectives. I look forward to working with my Executive colleagues to agree how we can best achieve this and ensure that we have a skills system that meets the needs of the economy and enables everyone to reach their full potential.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether his department will produce an action plan for the reform of careers advice.
answer_text: The provision of careers information, advice and guidance helps ensure that people, including those in schools, are fully informed about the opportunities available to them. My Department’s Careers Service supports individuals of any age, background or circumstance to make informed decisions. There are a range of published documents that include recommendations in relation to the role of the Careers Service. I will be engaging with officials and stakeholders on our work to enhance our careers advice as part of my aim to help more people access better jobs. Work will also be required with the Department of Education to ensure that schools provide all pupils with quality careers education.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether his department plans to fund the Skill Up initiative beyond its current funding arrangements.
answer_text: The Skill Up initiative has supported over 22,000 individuals since it was introduced in 2021. Having engaged with participants I am very aware of the positive impact that this has had for people right across the community. Skill Up is currently being evaluated and I will consider any new programme as part of the upcoming Budget exercise.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether his Department has plans to review and improve the Trummery Crossroads junction.
answer_text: Unfortunately my officials were unable to gather together the information sought by the member in the short amount of time I had remaining in office.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities whether Local Labour Market Partnerships are being utilised to address skills shortages in our local economy.
answer_text: The aim of Labour Market Partnerships (LMPs) is to bring together the best placed people within local areas to improve employability outcomes and labour market conditions by working in partnership to provide tailored provision to meet local needs. Improving the supply of skilled labour is one of the four key themes the Department for Communities has set for local LMPs to address within their annual Action Plans.LMPs have put in place a range of provision to assist citizens acquire the skills required to become active within the labour market.To date, over 2,000 qualifications have been achieved as a direct result of LMP provision, with over 300 people moving on to further education or training.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs what plans his Department has to address rural isolation and loneliness.
answer_text: My Departments Tackling Rural Poverty and Social Isolation (TRPSI) Framework focuses on three priority areas for intervention, namely Access Poverty, Financial Poverty and Social Isolation and aims to support initiatives designed to address these priorities in rural areas. A TRPSI budget for 2022/23 of £7.47m (£2.74m resource and £4.73m capital) is currently supporting a range of initiatives, which are consistent with the TRPSI Framework priorities. Details of these initiatives can be found at Initiatives to Tackle Rural Poverty and Social Isolation (TRPSI) | Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs (daera-ni.gov.uk) Along with the TRPSI Programme my Department continues to develop and deliver a number of pilot initiatives which have emanated from the work carried out to develop the new Rural Policy Framework. One such initiative is the Rural Halls Refurbishment Scheme which provides grants to rural community and voluntary organisations to deliver capital works to rural halls, with the aim of keeping community facilities sustainable and enhancing the range of activities they can deliver, thus contributing to addressing rural isolation and loneliness issues. To date, 74 Letters of Offer have issued to the value of £2.53m with a further 33 Letters of Offer to the value of £1.17m expected to issue to the remaining eligible applicants before the end of this year.A Loneliness Forum which the Department of Health facilitate and on which DAERA are represented, recognizes that loneliness is an issue that cannot be resolved by one department and therefore it aims to collaborate to tackle the issue. The Forum, consisting of central and local Government representatives, the 5 Health Trusts, Arm’s Length Bodies and representatives of the Voluntary Community Sector, continues to meet. The next scheduled meeting is later this month.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether his Department has given consideration to reassessing and evaluating how the wider Health and Social Care system recruits staff.
answer_text: My Department published the comprehensive Health and Social Care Workforce Strategy 2026: Delivering for Our People in May 2018.  The aim of the Strategy is that ‘by 2026, we meet our workforce needs and the needs of our workforce’.The Strategy’s second action plan, covering the period 2022/23 to 2024/25, was published on 15th June 2022 following detailed engagement with stakeholders from across the health and social care system.The second action plan contains a range of actions that address recruitment of staff.  These include the development of focussed campaigns to showcase and support recruitment into different health and social care professions; exploring new and alternative opportunities that may provide a recruitment and training pathway to a career in the health and social care system such as apprenticeship and trainee programmes; exploring opportunities to recruit health and social care professionals from other jurisdictions; establishing focused processes to facilitate the recruitment of staff returning to the HSC;  and exploring and establishing non salary incentive programmes as a means of recruiting staff including return on service commitments.The second action plan also contains a commitment to explore how we can improve the HSC recruitment model that not only reduces time taken to recruit but creates an improved experience for both candidates and recruiting managers. A full review of the HSC recruitment model and process is currently underway which will scope opportunities for improvement.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy how his Department will ensure that Levelling Up Funds and Shared Prosperity Funds will align with and complement economic priorities in Northern Ireland.
answer_text: Engagement on funding streams associated with the Levelling Up agenda is undertaken with the Department for Levelling Up, Housing and Communities (DLUHC), through the Department of Finance (DoF) led Future Policy & Finance Workstream.  My Department has provided extensive input and evidence to advocate for alignment & complementarity with NI economic priorities. Ultimately the decisions on spending will be made by DLUHC.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education whether every post-primary school is made aware of, and promotes, apprenticeships for young people as a career opportunity.
answer_text: Careers education is embedded in the Northern Ireland Curriculum through the Employability strand of Learning for Life and Work (LLW). A requirement of this strand of LLW is that pupils should be enabled to develop a personal career plan based on relevant information and guidance. The Careers Service, which is managed by the Department for the Economy, provides impartial careers information, advice and guidance and works in partnership with schools to support young people to make decisions at key transition points. All pupils will be offered at least one face-to-face careers guidance interview with a Careers Service Advisor. This interview usually takes place in Year 12 and the purpose of the careers guidance interview is to help pupils consider their career options.  Each post-primary school in Northern Ireland is a member of one of 27 Area Learning Communities (ALCs) organised geographically across NI. Each ALC has a careers sub-group comprising of careers teachers and ALC coordinators from the participating schools. The aim is to share best practice, and to develop joint events for their pupils. The Education Authority has coordinated an ALC Conference to be held on 9 November 2022.  The programme for the conference contains a presentation by Workplus on ‘The Easier Way to Find an Apprenticeship’. Workplus is an organisation that helps to match employers and apprentices.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education whether the former Dromore Central Primary site is to be used as a special needs site.
answer_text: The Education Authority have advised that it continues to retain the former Dromore Central Primary School site for proposed future educational use.  One of the options under consideration is for the provision of specialist school provision.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy (i) how many people in his Department are working full-time on the energy strategy; and (ii) when his Department started preparatory work on supporting consumers and businesses with energy costs.
answer_text: Within Energy Group, there are currently 76 full time equivalent staff contributing to the development and delivery of policy for Energy Strategy.  In addition, delivering the Energy Strategy utilises the contributions of staff from other DfE Groups such as Skills and Education and work being done by staff in other government departments such as DAERA, DfI, DoF and DfC.  Of the 76, there are 2 members of staff in the core Energy Strategy team responsible for the management of the development and delivery of the Energy Strategy itself across government. Their current focus includes reporting on the Energy Strategy Action Plan 2022 and production of the 2023 Action Plan. In terms of energy costs, I have welcomed the range of supports which have been announced by the UK Government. I have called for these supports to be delivered directly by the UK Government to consumers here. The Department for Business, Energy and Industrial Strategy are engaging directly with the NI Utility Regulator and the local energy companies with my officials supporting as technical observers. I look forward to these supports being implemented as quickly as possible.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether his Department has plans to include St Joseph's Primary School, Lisburn in a 20mph School Speed Limit Zone.
answer_text: Delivery of the second tranche of part-time 20mph speed limits at schools is at an advanced stage and upon completion 215 schools across the North will be benefitting from this initiative. When all of the tranche 2 sites are fully operational and have had sufficient time to bed in, my Department intends to carry out a review of this work to determine if delivery of a further tranche is justified. Until this review is carried out I am not in a position to commit to the installation part-time 20mph speed limits any further schools, including St Joseph’s Primary School, Lisburn.Should you have any further questions on this matter, please contact DfI Roads Eastern Division at: dfiroads.eastern@infrastructure-ni.gov.uk.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on getting the yellow lines extended at the Hillsborough Road end of Warren Park, Lisburn.
answer_text: I can confirm that my Department proposes to provide waiting restrictions, double yellow lines along a section of Warren Park towards Hillsborough Road. While the legislation process is nearing its final stages, as part of the legislative process final checks are being carried out and a submission is being prepared for my consideration.  Once I have received and considered that submission I will take a decision as to whether to proceed with this legislation. Should you have any further questions on this matter, please contact DfI Roads Eastern Division at: dfiroads.eastern@infrastructure-ni.gov.uk.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy what discussions his Department has had with the Department of Health on issues of Workforce Planning and if there is a joint strategy to address the issue.
answer_text: Workforce Planning for the health service is the strategic responsibility of the Department of Health.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on what work his Department has undertaken on the Mutual Recognition of Professional Qualifications.
answer_text: Recognition of professional qualifications is a cross-cutting issue, with a number of Departments holding policy responsibility for regulated professions. Officials in my Department, along with colleagues in The Executive Office, co-chair a cross-Departmental Working Group on recognition of professional qualifications. My officials also act as the primary point of contact for the Department for Business, Energy and Industrial Strategy (BEIS), the lead Department in Whitehall, and ensure they are aware of NI views on a number of work areas, including the recent Professional Qualifications Act, the development of common frameworks and the functioning of the Common Travel Area.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education how many schools are availing of animal assisted therapy or have a school pet that is used to support emotional health and wellbeing.
answer_text: Unfortunately my Department does not collate information on how many schools are availing of animal assisted therapy or have a school pet that is used to support emotional health and wellbeing.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education how many primary schools in NI offer free music tuition to pupils using school funds to pay for the lessons.
answer_text: My Department does not hold data on whether individual schools offer free music tuition.                         The Education Authority (EA) provides a Music Service for schools.  If pupils in a school are receiving music tuition via the Music Service, the school is charged directly for the tutor’s time.  The school may pass on all, part or none of the charge to parents, depending on its policy.  This policy, however, must be applied in accordance with section 19 of the Governor Guide which covers ‘School Fees, Charges and Remissions’.  Schools are not charged for pupils who are in receipt of Free School Meals for the following, through the Music Service:the hiring of a musical instrument;attending lessons with the Music Service in the City of Belfast School of Music, if there is no Music Service tuition available in their  school; andmembership of and attending a Music Service orchestra, band or ensemble.As with all areas of the curriculum, it is a matter for schools to decide which resources, including music resources, to utilise in order to meet the needs of their pupils.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs whether his Department has a plan to protect the Irish Black Bee and how the effectiveness of such a plan will be monitored.
answer_text: My Department has no specific plan for the protection of the Irish Black Bee. However, my Department is a partner organisation and is contributing to the All-Ireland Pollinator Plan which includes the All-Ireland Honeybee Strategy 2021-2025. This strategy seeks to enhance the conservation of honeybees, including the native Irish Black Bee.  The strategy recognises the role of the Bee-keeping sector in education on the merits of the subspecies and associated its husbandry.  DAERA and AFBI are represented on a steering group, which monitors the progress of the Pollinator Plan.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to outline the current status of the River Lagan, in line with the water framework directive.
answer_text: The River Lagan is comprised of six waterbodies. The most recent assessment of river water quality published by my Department in 2021, assessed the Lagan waterbodies upstream of Lisburn as moderate ecological status and the river water body which comprises the stretch between Lisburn and Belfast as poor ecological status. All the Lagan river waterbodies were assessed as moderate chemical status.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether his Department is doing any preparatory work around potential economic turbulence, given the economic forecasting.
answer_text: My Department monitors the economic environment closely and seeks to provide appropriate responses to assist businesses and individuals. The Northern Ireland economy has bounced back well from the Covid pandemic, helped by the wide range of interventions that my Department brought forward. However, it is clear that economies around the globe are now facing a number of economic challenges including those associated with inflationary pressures. Those pressures have implications for the cost of living and the cost of doing business and there is evidence that they are denting consumer and business confidence. While the key drivers of inflation lie largely outside of my Department’s control, we have strategies in place to provide a roadmap for the short term response, and longer term economic prosperity of Northern Ireland.Last year the Department launched the ambitious 10X economic vision which aims to make the Northern Ireland economy ten times better through a decade of innovation. This will deliver an economy for the benefit of all our people – an economy which is more inclusive, more innovative, and more sustainable.In parallel to that 10X vision, in March I launched ‘Skills for a 10X Economy’, which will seek to invest in the skills and talent of our workforce and unlock future economic prosperity for everyone.Furthermore, my ambitious Energy Strategy – ‘The Path to Net Zero Energy’ – sets out a vision to deliver self-sufficiency in affordable, renewable energy for Northern Ireland.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy (i) what work his Department is doing, in conjunction with the Department for Communities, around energising our town and urban centres; and (ii) what the outcome of this work has been to date.
answer_text: My Department, along with the Department for Communities (DfC) and other Executive Departments, is represented on the High Street Task Force (HSTF). The HSTF was established by the Executive Office (TEO) to deliver an agreed vision for sustainable city, town, and village centres.  It is co-chaired by TEO’s Junior Ministers.The HSTF produced its report in March 2022, containing 14 recommendations, many of which are cross-cutting. The report, “Delivering a 21st Century High Street”, will require consideration by the new Executive.DfC, working closely with local councils, has lead responsibility for town regeneration, whilst other departments have responsibilities for planning and licensing etc.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure why his Department does not consider St Michael's Primary School, Finnis, to be suitable for the 20mph speed limit scheme.
answer_text: I can confirm that St Michael's Primary School, Finnis was assessed along with all other schools within the Newry, Mourne & Down area using the assessment framework. The highest scoring schools were included in tranche one with the next highest ranking schools included in second tranche. On the basis of its assessment score St Michael’s Primary School did not rank highly enough to warrant inclusion in either tranche 1 or 2.The second tranche of part-time 20mph speed limits at schools is being delivered this year and upon completion, over 215 schools across the North will be benefitting from this initiative. When all of the tranche 2 sites are fully operational and have had sufficient time to bed in, my Department intends to carry out a review of this work to determine if a further tranche is justified. Until this review is carried out I am not in a position to commit to install part-time 20mph speed limits at any more schools, including St Michael’s Primary School, Finnis.Should you have any further questions on this matter, please contact DfI Roads -Southern Division at: dfiroads.southern@infrastructure-ni.gov.uk.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy (i) what work his Department is doing to encourage and promote green jobs and the green economy; and (ii) whether this workstream is fully funded.
answer_text: Invest NI’s work in the Green Economy focuses on two key areas:Identifying new market opportunities in the green economy and ensuring that companies, across key clusters, are supported in competing for these global opportunities.Supporting NI businesses to go greener, focusing on green efficiencies and decarbonisation.New Market Opportunities Invest NI is committed to driving green investment across Invest NI products and solutions. A major study on Green Growth opportunities has identified the overarching areas of opportunity energy generation, low carbon heat and transport. Flowing from this, three opportunities have been identified for Northern Ireland, Offshore Wind, Green Hydrogen and Energy Efficiency including retrofit. These areas will be a focus for Invest NI going forward and are vital for delivering against the 10X vision, the Executive’s Green Growth Strategy, DfE’s Energy Strategy and Invest NI’s strategic opportunities.Invest NI will position NI company capability to enable them to take advantage of green opportunities in new markets through its Trade Support. Invest NI will also work in key international markets to attract investment in the key areas of offshore wind, green hydrogen and energy efficiency and work with the local ecosystem to development of targeted assets and events to promote these opportunities.Supporting NI businesses to go greenerInvest NI delivers a suite of Energy and Resource Efficiency support for businesses, including specialist advice and investment capital, to enable NI businesses to become more efficient and resilient through green competences. This includes: fully funded Technical Consultancy; a Resource Matching Service; the Resource Efficiency Capital Grant; and, sustainability reports.This support helps businesses to optimise and futureproof their operations by implementing green efficiencies to achieve cost savings and carbon savings.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure what plans his Department has to address road safety issues on the Brokerstown Road, Lisburn.
answer_text: I am advised that officials from Roads Eastern Division are not aware of any specific road safety issues on Brokerstown Road in Lisburn. It is however acknowledged that the road layout has experienced some changes in recent times due to housing development at both the Knockmore Road and Ballymacash Road ends.My Department is in the progress of introducing a part-time 20mph speed limit at Ballymacash Primary school as part of the rollout of tranche 2 of the part-time 20mph at schools programme. Work is well underway and should be completed in advance of the start of the new school year in September 2022. Officials also plan to review the existing speed limit along the road in association with the PSNI.Should you have any further questions on these matters, please contact DfI Roads Eastern at: dfiroads.eastern@infrastructure-ni.gov.uk.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy (i) what action his Department has taken to promote Northern Ireland's position in having access to both UK and EU Markets; and (ii) how many companies have cited this as a reason for investing in Northern Ireland.
answer_text: (i)  Northern Ireland is a great place to work and do business because of our key strengths including a talented and loyal workforce, advanced telecoms infrastructure, a pro-business environment, and competitive operating costs. My Department, along with Invest NI, continues to promote this message around the world. The detail of the messaging used to promote investment in NI is tailored to the interests of the particular companies and investors involved. (ii)  Businesses express a range of reasons for investing in NI, including for our key strengths set out above. Investment discussions are commercially sensitive therefore I am not in a position to disclose the specific content of individual discussions.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether his Department has any plans to regulate zero hours contracts as per the provisions in the Employment Act (Northern Ireland) 2016.
answer_text: My Department will consider the matter of zero hours contracts in the context of wider employment law issues and will bring forward proposals for consideration in due course.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy (i) what his department is doing to achieve a culture of lifelong learning as part of the Skills Strategy; (ii) how this will be achieved across sectors; and (iii) whether a cross-departmental approach will be taken.
answer_text: My Department’s new Skills Strategy, ‘Skills for a 10x Economy’, has lifelong learning at its heart and highlights the need to improve access to education and training opportunities, by alleviating cost and time constraints, both of which I appreciate are key concerns for those with caring responsibilities.My officials are working with colleagues in other Departments, including the Department for Communities and Department of Education; to scope out recommendations that would support a lifelong learning Action Plan. The initial focus will be to increase awareness of the Departments existing provision and improving access to learning opportunities.  The Northern Ireland Skills Council has recently been established as an advisory body, providing strategic advice to Executive Ministers and government departments, on issues pertaining to the development of skills policy.  The Council will bring together senior policymakers, business leaders, representatives of the education sector and trade unions to provide ongoing advice and support as the new Skills Strategy is implemented.  It will also support and encourage the continuation of the whole-of-government approach ensuring a more cohesive, collaborative approach to implementing the new Strategy which includes the Lifelong Learning Action Plan. Industry representation on the Skills Council provides a focal point for engagement across sectors, ensuring implementation plans agile and responsive to existing and emerging labour market need.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for his assessment of the impact that working on an all-island basis, including for infrastructure, digital connectivity and innovation, will have in addressing key economic issues.
answer_text: Collaboration, where mutually beneficial, can be advantageous across a range of sectors. 		I recognise the importance of bilateral and multilateral research and innovation collaboration in general, not just on a North / South or East / West basis, but also with other international partners. This collaboration is critically important as a means of accelerating innovation and research in support of a strong and competitive economy.  		One such existing example is the long standing US/Ireland R&D Partnership which gives researchers in Northern Ireland institutions unique access to research collaboration opportunities with US as well as Irish Republic based institutions. The Department for the Economy provides up to £2 million each year to support the Northern Ireland universities’ participation in the Partnership.		The Department also supports InterTradeIreland (ITI). ITI works to promote, develop and expand cross-border trade by SMEs which contributes to increased efficiency and innovation (including the development of new products, processes or services). ITI supports businesses through several programmes including FUSION/Innovation Boost, Acumen and Impact.		With regards to energy infrastructure, my Department supports the construction of the North/South Interconnector because of the contribution it will make to increasing electricity trading, encouraging new investment in generation and supply, and enhancing Northern Ireland’s security of supply position.  In addition, it is essential to facilitate a significantly increased contribution to Northern Ireland’s power generation from renewable sources.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health (i) what the average length of time is for someone to be diagnosed with endometriosis in the South Eastern Health and Social Care Trust; and (ii) what support is available from the Trust for people diagnosed with endometriosis.
answer_text: Gynaecology referrals to the South Eastern Trust Outpatient service are not recorded by diagnosis. The Trust is therefore unable to separate patients referred with suspected endometriosis from the total number of gynaecology referrals received. It is therefore not possible to determine the average length of time is for someone to be diagnosed with endometriosis as this will be specific to each patient.Patients who are diagnosed with endometriosis are referred to Endometriosis UK for support.  This charity has branches in Belfast and Derry.  Endometriosis UK provide monthly support sessions with guest speakers to include Gynae Consultants, Specialist Nurses, Patient Client Council & Anaesthetics for Pain Management.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs what steps his Department is taking to influence the curriculum to inform young people about native species and the identification of native species.
answer_text: At present my Department is not directly involved in influencing the curriculum to inform young people about native species and identification. However the Department does provide funding to a range of organisations to progress educational initiatives including provision of teacher training, teaching materials and site based activities that enhance young people’s knowledge and awareness of biodiversity and species.  One such example is the funding support provided to Keep Northern Ireland Beautiful to help deliver the Eco-Schools programme.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs when the Northern Ireland Priority Species List was last reviewed.
answer_text: The current Northern Ireland Priority Species List was published in 2010, following a UK-wide review.  However review of component species groups is an ongoing process, and the list is being reviewed by Departmental officials.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy how many people are availing of an apprenticeship in the Lagan Valley constituency, broken down by (i) sector; and (ii) gender.
answer_text: My Department provides funding through the ApprenticeshipsNI (AppsNI) and Higher Level Apprenticeships (HLA) programmes for the delivery of off the job training for apprenticeships at levels 2 to 7.According to figures for ApprenticeshipsNI levels 2 to 3, occupancy by sector and gender in Lagan Valley Area at 31 October 2021 is as follows:SectorFemaleMaleTotalAccounting and Finance6511Administration303Agriculture224Building and Construction2113115Child Development and Well Being516Engineering	28688Health and Social Care14014Horticulture, Forestry011Hospitality and Catering202141Information Communication Technology Practitioners23335Manufacturing Technologies53944Publishing, Information Services033Retailing and Wholesaling358Services Enterprises617Transport Operations and Maintenance14344Warehousing/Distribution033Total71356427The breakdown of figures for HLA levels 4 to 5 for the academic year 2020/21 is as follows:SectorFemaleMaleTotalHealth, Public Services and Care13013Science and Mathematics022Engineering and Manufacturing Technologies12223Construction, Planning and the Built Environment257Information and Communication Technology055Retail and Commercial Enterprise011Business, Administration and Law707Total233558Finally, there were 30 participants enrolled on a HLA level 6-7 programme for the academic year 2020/21 within the Lagan Valley constituency. This is broken down to 15 within Computing and 15 within Business Management. Of these 30 participants, 10 were female and 20 were male.Data is based on the home postcode of each apprentice.  Apprentices may have an employment base either inside or outside the Lagan Valley constituency.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education (i) what her Department intends to do with the former Dromore Central Primary School site; and (ii) why the site has been vacant for a long period.
answer_text: The Education Authority continues to retain the former Dromore Central PS site for proposed future educational use.  One of the options under consideration is for the provision of specialist school services. Until recently there was engagement with the Health Trust on the potential of this site being used for a shared scheme to relocate the local GP surgery and Dromore Nursery School. However due to funding issues and different priorities for respective capital programmes the proposal did not progress. As per point (i) above the EA is now actively considering the site for use by other potential EA services.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the waiting times for young people to (i) receive an ADHD diagnosis; and (ii) access ADHD support, in the South Eastern Health and Social Care Trust.
answer_text: In the South Eastern Health and Social Care (HSC) Trust, the current waiting time for a new patient assessment for ADHD, from the date of referral, is 560 days. In the South Eastern Health and Social Care (HSC) Trust, parents/carers are contacted on average around six months before the assessment date to distribute resources and to signpost community based support services.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs whether his Department plans to make the microchipping and registration of cats mandatory.
answer_text: I am aware that the Department for the Environment, Food and Rural Affairs (Defra) intends to take forward legislation to make the microchipping of cats in England mandatory.  My Department’s Code of Practice for the Welfare of Cats already recommends that owners ensure their cats are identified using either collars or microchips. At this juncture, my Department has not identified any immediate need to make the microchipping of cats compulsory in Northern Ireland. It will however, continue to keep the progression and implementation of the relevant Defra legislation under review.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether his Department plans to conduct an audit of people living with diabetes to better inform services and outcomes.
answer_text: In Northern Ireland, the Diabetes Network brings together people living with diabetes, carers, health and social care professionals, clinicians and Diabetes UK (NI) to work in partnership on the design and delivery of better diabetes services.  The Diabetes Network is supportive of an audit in respect of diabetes in Northern Ireland. Network representatives have met with representatives from Diabetes UK to discuss the National Diabetes Audit and data requirements.  The Diabetes Network will continue to work with Diabetes UK to scope and consider what is possible to support an audit approach in Northern Ireland.  This will include consideration of what form such an audit would take.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health what support is available for people with pulmonary fibrosis and their families.
answer_text: Pulmonary fibrosis falls under Interstitial Lung Disease (ILD) and is incurable.  Several measures help relieve symptoms and can slow progression including: stopping smoking, eating healthily and exercising regularly; antifibrotic medicines such as pirfenidone and nintedanib; home oxygen therapy; and pulmonary rehabilitation. Patients with pulmonary fibrosis are diagnosed and managed through Health and Social Care (HSC) Trusts’ multi-disciplinary teams.  The patient and their families are given all the available education and information to assist in managing this condition and are made aware of the voluntary groups that also provide excellent advice and support.Trust ILD nurse specialists work with pharmacists to provide information and support to people with pulmonary fibrosis and their families and carers. Patients have access to nurse-led assessments for oxygen therapy and pulmonary rehabilitation.  The Home Oxygen service assesses the need for oxygen therapy and regularly reviews patients as very high flows of oxygen therapy is often required as the condition worsens.  During acute flare-ups patients may be admitted to a respiratory specialist ward.  Where patients are being considered for lung transplant, a monthly clinic assesses patients.  Where necessary Trusts also provide palliative care to patients with pulmonary fibrosis.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy how his Department is considering how to develop public sector apprenticeships, particularly in health and social care and procurement.
answer_text: From the Office of the Minister 	Gordon Lyons MLA								The Skills Strategy for Northern Ireland recognises that Public Sector Apprenticeships (PSAs) offer the opportunity for existing and prospective public servants to undertake high quality learning and development as part of their work.  There are already a number of PSAs in place across the public sector including a Procurement Apprenticeship in Construction and Procurement Delivery operated by the Department of Finance.  Initial discussions have taken place with the Department of Health on potential apprenticeship opportunities in the health and social care sector.  However, the Minister of Finance’s proposed draft Budget for 2022-25 has prevented the embedding of PSAs within the Department for the Economy’s apprenticeship programmes as it did not invest in skills, education, innovation or prioritise the economy.   Nevertheless, this Department will work collaboratively with partners across the public sector to facilitate PSA knowledge sharing, best practise and opportunities to ensure alignment between public and private sector pathways and talent development.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy what actions his Department is taking to advance all age apprenticeships.
answer_text: My Department continues to work towards increasing the supply of skills to the economy and widening the range of apprenticeship opportunities available to individuals of all ages. I remain committed to the principle of reforming age-related eligibility criteria for the ApprenticeshipsNI programme at the earliest opportunity.  Such reform is dependent on future budget availability.  However, the Minister of Finance’s proposed draft Budget for 2022-25 has prevented this initiative progressing as it did not invest in skills, education, innovation or prioritise the economy.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy what actions his Department is taking to engage with the Department of Education on the 14-19 policy.
answer_text: My Department works closely with the Department of Education on a wide range of issues, including 14-19 policy.  The “Transition of Young People into Careers (14-19) Project” was established in January 2019 to take forward the development of a more strategic approach to 14-19 education and training.  The Project is jointly led by my Department and the Department of Education with joint governance structures.  The Project has developed a draft strategic framework document which sets out a range of proposed actions to transform the 14-19 education and training landscape.  The draft Framework is currently being finalised and the Minister of Education and I hope to publish this before the end of June.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to outline the timescales for the rollout of multi-disciplinary teams in the South Eastern Health and Social Care Trust.
answer_text: There are currently around 340 whole time equivalent front line staff working across 101 GP practices in 7 GP Federation areas in the Primary Care MDT programme. Over a quarter of these staff work or are attached to 21 GP practices in the Down, North Down and Ards areas of the South Eastern Health and Social Care Trust.  The continuing rollout of the MDT Programme is a key priority for my Department and remains critical to stabilising and strengthening vital Primary Care services in the community.  Work is underway on plans for the further rollout of the MDT model, subject to the availability of funding.I am very conscious of pressing demands for extra funding across many areas of health and social care. As Minister, I will continue to do the best I can to deliver for the public with the resources I have available.Amid ongoing budget uncertainty, I am on record as warning that funding pressures in health may be significant by the second half of 2022/23. The financial situation will undoubtedly be constrained whatever the final budget settlement. My ability to plan strategically, including on the further rollout of the MDT Programme, is being significantly impaired by the ongoing budgetary uncertainty.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy how his Department plans to address rising energy prices in both the gas and electricity markets.
answer_text: High energy prices remain a significant concern for households and businesses as ongoing volatility in international wholesale fuel prices continue to impact on bills across the whole of Europe. While various factors are contributing to the high energy prices, the main driver is the surge in the price of natural gas driven by the conflict between Russia and Ukraine. While the UK gets very little gas from Russia, UK gas prices are linked to the global markets. Disruptions in European gas supplies from Russia have increased global prices which in turn has driven up the price we pay in the UK.  The price of gas is a key driver in setting wholesale electricity prices.The new Energy Strategy sets out the intention to introduce minimum standards for the energy efficiency of buildings and to substantially increase funding and support for retrofitting buildings. This will help consumers make changes that reduce their energy use and subsequently the amount they need to spend on energy. The Chancellor’s announcement on 26 May 2022 outlined a range of cost-of-living support packages for households throughout the UK. The DfE Energy Team is engaging with the Department of Finance (DoF) to understand the application of this support in Northern Ireland.My Department continues to have an oversight role in the Utility Regulator’s (UR) regulation of gas tariffs charged by the dominant suppliers in the Greater Belfast and Ten Towns license areas and also the largest electricity supplier in Northern Ireland. This ensures that customers pay no more than the efficient costs of purchasing and supplying the energy plus an agreed profit margin set by the UR.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether his Department plans to replace pitch fibre pipes within the NI Water network.
answer_text: The replacement of pitch fibre pipes within the NI Water network across the North is an operational matter for NI Water.  You should therefore, direct your question to NI Water at waterline@niwater.com

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy what correspondence his Department has had to date with the Trader Support Service.
answer_text: Departmental officials have corresponded with the Trader Support Service (TSS) on hundreds of occasions to assist local businesses. This has been to raise issues brought forward by the business community and seek resolution; as well as to understand and communicate the many upgrades TSS has introducd.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy what his Department is doing in relation to the regulation of home heating oil.
answer_text: Given the recognised competitive market in home heating, oil there is no consideration being given to regulating the sector. 				If we were to take such intervention, it would require primary legislation to establish the regulatory framework.  At the present time, the Competition and Markets Authority has jurisdiction to investigate and take action to address anti-competitive practices. 				I am sure the Member will also be familiar with my energy strategy; The Path to Net Zero Energy, which sets out a pathway to decarbonising our society and gradually phasing out fossil fuels. My immediate focus is on ensuring delivery of the action plan which I launched earlier this year.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on the upgrade of the junction at The Cutts, Derriaghy, Lisburn.
answer_text: My Department is very aware of public concerns about right-turn facilities at the junction of McKinstry Road and The Cutts. The solution identified to address this issue involved fully controlling the right turn movements within the signal phasing and I am pleased to advise that the detailed design for such a scheme has recently been completed and land acquisition is also progressing.Officials are currently progressing with preparation of the contract documents for this scheme for which costs are currently estimated at £2.4m. Subject to availability of resources, this will allow the Department to take the scheme forward upon satisfactory completion of the land acquisition process.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy how the money paid by employers through the Apprenticeship Levy is being spent.
answer_text: The apprenticeship levy is a reserved matter and the Department for the Economy has no remit over either the paying or allocation of levy funds.  HM Treasury distribute a Barnett consequential from the levy to the Devolved Administrations.  Skills and training are devolved matters, and each administration has developed their own training and apprenticeship strategies and funding mechanisms.  The off-the-job element of apprenticeship training in Northern Ireland is funded through my Department’s ApprenticeshipsNI and Higher Level Apprenticeship programmes.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether his Department will reform the Apprenticeship Levy.
answer_text: The apprenticeship levy is a reserved matter and the Department for the Economy has no remit over either the paying or allocation of levy funds.  HM Treasury distribute a Barnett consequential from the levy to the Devolved Administrations.  Skills and training are devolved matters, and each administration has developed their own training and apprenticeship strategies and funding mechanisms.  The off-the-job element of apprenticeship training in Northern Ireland is funded through my Department’s ApprenticeshipsNI and Higher Level Apprenticeship programmes.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities whether her Department is engaged with the Department of Health to better understand the relationship between access to housing and good health outcomes.
answer_text: There are fundamental links between access to safe, affordable housing and good health outcomes.  The strong links between good housing and good mental and physical health are explicitly recognised in my Draft Housing Supply Strategy and indeed the Department of Health is represented on the Housing Supply Strategy Project Board; the prevention of homelessness, reducing housing stress and prioritising housing solutions for those most in need being the main focus for this work.  By providing supported housing my Department and the Department of Health improve health outcomes for some of our most vulnerable citizens.  Both Departments and the Housing Executive co-operate through an Interdepartmental Housing and Health Adaptations Programme Board.  The IHHAPB promotes collaborative working between those agencies in the delivery of housing for people with disabilities, older and young people who may need extra support to live independently. It also provides a platform for the relevant stakeholders to engage and identify areas to collaborate, and improve service delivery and outcomes across housing tenure.Another particular area where housing and health are deeply intertwined is homelessness.  The Housing Executive engages with the Department for Communities and Department of Health to support the delivery of the Year 3 Inter Departmental Homelessness Action Plan (IDHAP), which was published in May 2022 and seeks to address non-accommodation based issues relevant to homelessness. The IDHAP sits alongside the Homelessness Strategy 2022-27 and seeks to address accommodation based issues relevant to homelessness.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether his Department has any plans to (i) promote Green apprenticeships; and (ii) incentivise employers wishing to offer Green apprenticeships.
answer_text: Apprenticeship frameworks are available across a range of levels and sectors to underpin the 10X Economy Vision to support a greener, sustainable economy.  These include Level 2 and Level 3 apprenticeships in Electrical Power Engineering, Electrotechnical, Engineering, Environmental Conservation, Plumbing & Heating and Wind Turbine Operations And Higher Level Apprenticeships are available in Building Services with Sustainable Energy, Energy, Environment and Sustainability, and Mechanical Engineering.		Employer-led Sectoral Partnerships, which operate across a wide range of sectors, are responsible for ensuring that apprenticeship frameworks meet the 	current and evolving needs of industry.  When enhancing our current apprenticeships, Sectoral Partnerships are encouraged to ensure that they meet the needs of the employers within the growing green economy and, where required, consider creating new apprenticeships to reflect new occupations.	As an incentive for employers to employ apprentices, funding for the off-the job training element of an apprenticeship is paid by the Department to the training provider.  Also, under the ApprenticeshipsNI programme an Employer Incentive Payment is already available for employers whose apprentice successfully completes their full Level 2 or Level 3 apprenticeships framework.  This incentive varies depending on the complexity and level of the apprenticeship undertaken and the age of the apprentice on joining the programme.  There are no plans to make this incentive payment sector specific.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities whether people presenting as homeless to the Housing Executive are signposted to organisations providing emotional or mental health support.
answer_text: The Housing Executive, provides person centred services which are tailored to meet the needs of individuals, supports them to achieve sustainable housing solutions and, to prevent homelessness where possible, draws upon the necessary support from other agencies to support clients.This will include signposting to organisations providing emotional or mental health support including, but not limited to, those supported by the Housing Executive’s Supporting People programme.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities how many participants in the JobStart programme have been offered employment after it has ended; and whether the offer of employment was (i) part-time; and (ii) full-time.
answer_text: As of 31 May 2022, 391 young people had completed their JobStart opportunity. I am pleased to confirm that, of those, 188 young people have been retained by their JobStart employer and a further 97 have successfully found alternative employment as a direct result of the skills and experience acquired during their time on the JobStart Scheme. This represents an impressive 73% “into employment” rate at participant Scheme end date.   I cannot advise in terms of full time or part time employment status as my officials do not collect such data.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail the number of (i) recruits to the JobStart programme; (ii) employers signed up to offer places; and (iii) places registered.
answer_text: At 30 April 2022, 1,319 young people had commenced a JobStart opportunity. Since the JobStart Scheme launched on 2 April 2021, 2,202 employers made applications to the JobStart Scheme, offering 6,772 JobStart places.  My Department approved 4,111 of these places, which provided a wide range of jobs and opportunities for young people.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on (i) the A1 road safety improvement scheme; (ii) the status of the economic appraisal; and (iii) the envisaged timescale for procurement and completion.
answer_text: I am fully committed to delivering this important road safety project. The statutory orders for the scheme were made in summer 2021 and work on the development of the business case is nearing completion. The next stage is to prepare the procurement and contract documents. This work will take approximately 12 months and be funded as part of the financial contingency arrangements. However, the continued refusal of the DUP to form an Executive and an agreed 3-year Budget causes uncertainty in terms of timescales for commencing the tender process and moving to construction.
